title: Modern Wood Side Table
summary: A minimal two-piece side table — two 1x12 sides notched to interlock at the center, capped with a single 1x6 shelf resting in the notch.
description:
This is about as few parts as a side table can have: two solid 1x12 panels, each notched halfway up, slot together at the center to form a simple X-cross base — no screws or nails hold the two main panels to each other at that joint, the notch itself is what keeps them square and standing.

The single 1x6 shelf rests across the notch where the two panels cross, nailed and glued in place from both sides once the base is standing. **Cut the notches carefully to a snug, not sloppy, fit** — a loose notch is the one thing that would make this minimal design wobble.

With only three boards total, this is a fast build, but the payoff is in the finish — sand every edge smooth and take care with the stain or paint, since there's no drawer or apron to distract from a rough surface.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Each notched halfway up to interlock at the center.

steps.title: Cut the notches
steps.body: Mark and cut a snug notch halfway up each cross panel so the two interlock cleanly at the center.

steps.title: Assemble the base
steps.body: Slot the two panels together at the notches to form the X-cross base.

steps.title: Attach the shelf
steps.body: Glue and nail the shelf across the top of the notched crossing.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand every edge smooth and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Minimalist modern wood side table with X-cross base
